name: gitlab
explanation: GitLab exposes Prometheus metrics on the main app at `/-/metrics`, but the endpoint is IP-allowlisted by default (`monitoring.ip_whitelist` in gitlab.rb) — the Agent host must be allowed before the URL works. Additionally there are component-specific endpoints (Gitaly on 9236, sidekiq exporter, postgres exporter, etc.) which the check supports via separate fields. The legacy `prometheus_url` and the v2 `openmetrics_endpoint` paths trigger different implementations (gitlab.py vs gitlab_v2.py). Auto-config must probe `/-/metrics` and degrade gracefully when the allowlist blocks the Agent; the `api_token` for version metadata is optional but typically required in production.
notes: All instance fields are technically optional in the spec, but the check requires at least one of openmetrics_endpoint / prometheus_url / prometheus_endpoint. Multiple deployment shapes (omnibus on a single host vs. distributed components on dedicated ports) make a single canonical probe insufficient.
